```go
import "github.com/gen2brain/malgo"
```

<a name="IOSSessionCategoryOptionMixWithOthers"></a>IOSSessionCategoryOptions enumeration.

```go
const (
    IOSSessionCategoryOptionMixWithOthers                        = 0x01 // AVAudioSessionCategoryOptionMixWithOthers
    IOSSessionCategoryOptionDuckOthers                           = 0x02 // AVAudioSessionCategoryOptionDuckOthers
    IOSSessionCategoryOptionAllowBluetooth                       = 0x04 // AVAudioSessionCategoryOptionAllowBluetooth
    IOSSessionCategoryOptionDefaultToSpeaker                     = 0x08 // AVAudioSessionCategoryOptionDefaultToSpeaker
    IOSSessionCategoryOptionInterruptSpokenAudioAndMixWithOthers = 0x11 // AVAudioSessionCategoryOptionInterruptSpokenAudioAndMixWithOthers
    IOSSessionCategoryOptionAllowBluetoothA2dp                   = 0x20 // AVAudioSessionCategoryOptionAllowBluetoothA2DP
    IOSSessionCategoryOptionAllowAirPlay                         = 0x40 // AVAudioSessionCategoryOptionAllowAirPlay
)
```

<a name="AllocatedContext"></a>

## type [AllocatedContext](<https://github.com/verkada/malgo/blob/master/context.go#L173-L176>)

AllocatedContext is a Context that has been created by the application. It must be freed after use in order to release resources.

```go
type AllocatedContext struct {
    Context
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="InitContext"></a>

### func [InitContext](<https://github.com/verkada/malgo/blob/master/context.go#L180>)

```go
func InitContext(backends []Backend, config ContextConfig, logProc LogProc) (*AllocatedContext, error)
```

InitContext creates and initializes a context. When the application no longer needs the context instance, it needs to call Free\(\) .

<a name="AllocatedContext.Free"></a>

### func \(\*AllocatedContext\) [Free](<https://github.com/verkada/malgo/blob/master/context.go#L215>)

```go
func (ctx *AllocatedContext) Free()
```

Free must be called when the allocated data is no longer used. This function must only be called for an uninitialized context.

Context is used for selecting and initializing the relevant backends.

```go
type Context struct {
```

<a name="DefaultContext"></a>DefaultContext is an unspecified context. It can be used to initialize a streaming function with implicit context defaults.

```go
var DefaultContext Context = Context{}
```

<a name="Device"></a>

## type [Device](<https://github.com/verkada/malgo/blob/master/device.go#L25-L27>)

```go
type Device struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
```

<a name="InitDevice"></a>

### func [InitDevice](<https://github.com/verkada/malgo/blob/master/device.go#L38>)

```go
func InitDevice(context Context, deviceConfig DeviceConfig, deviceCallbacks DeviceCallbacks) (*Device, error)
```

InitDevice initializes a device.

The device ID can be nil, in which case the default device is used. Otherwise, you can retrieve the ID by calling Context.Devices\(\) and use the ID from the returned data.

Set device ID to nil to use the default device. Do \_not\_ rely on the first device ID returned by Context.Devices\(\) to be the default device.

The returned instance has to be cleaned up using Uninit\(\).

<a name="Device.Start"></a>

### func \(\*Device\) [Start](<https://github.com/verkada/malgo/blob/master/device.go#L113>)

```go
func (dev *Device) Start() error
```

Start activates the device. For playback devices this begins playback. For capture devices it begins recording.

For a playback device, this will retrieve an initial chunk of audio data from the client before returning. The reason for this is to ensure there is valid audio data in the buffer, which needs to be done \_before\_ the device begins playback.

This API waits until the backend device has been started for real by the worker thread. It also waits on a mutex for thread\-safety.

<a name="Device.Stop"></a>

### func \(\*Device\) [Stop](<https://github.com/verkada/malgo/blob/master/device.go#L130>)

```go
func (dev *Device) Stop() error
```

Stop puts the device to sleep, but does not uninitialize it. Use Start\(\) to start it up again.

This API needs to wait on the worker thread to stop the backend device properly before returning. It also waits on a mutex for thread\-safety. In addition, some backends need to wait for the device to finish playback/recording of the current fragment which can take some time \(usually proportionate to the buffer size that was specified at initialization time\).

<a name="Device.Type"></a>


<a name="Device.Uninit"></a>

### func \(\*Device\) [Uninit](<https://github.com/verkada/malgo/blob/master/device.go#L139>)

```go
func (dev *Device) Uninit()
```

Uninit uninitializes a device.

This will explicitly stop the device. You do not need to call Stop\(\) beforehand, but it's harmless if you do.

## type [DeviceCallbacks](<https://github.com/verkada/malgo/blob/master/device.go#L17-L22>)

DeviceCallbacks contains callbacks for one initialized device.

```go
type DeviceCallbacks struct {
    // Data is called for the full duplex IO.
    Data DataProc
    // Stop is called when the device stopped.
    Stop StopProc
}
```

```go
)
```

# capture

```go
import "github.com/gen2brain/malgo/_examples/capture"
```

This example simply captures data from your default microphone until you press Enter, after which it plays back the captured audio.

# enumeration

```go
import "github.com/gen2brain/malgo/_examples/enumeration"
```

## Index

# io\_api

```go
import "github.com/gen2brain/malgo/_examples/io_api"
```

## Index

- [func Capture\(ctx context.Context, w io.Writer, config StreamConfig\) error](<#Capture>)
- [func Playback\(ctx context.Context, r io.Reader, config StreamConfig\) error](<#Playback>)
- [type StreamConfig](<#StreamConfig>)

<a name="Capture"></a>

## func [Capture](<https://github.com/verkada/malgo/blob/master/_examples/io_api/capture.go#L15>)

```go
func Capture(ctx context.Context, w io.Writer, config StreamConfig) error
```

Capture records incoming samples into the provided writer. The function initializes a capture device in the default context using provide stream configuration. Capturing will commence writing the samples to the writer until either the writer returns an error, or the context signals done.

<a name="Playback"></a>

## func [Playback](<https://github.com/verkada/malgo/blob/master/_examples/io_api/playback.go#L15>)

```go
func Playback(ctx context.Context, r io.Reader, config StreamConfig) error
```

Playback streams samples from a reader to the sound device. The function initializes a playback device in the default context using provide stream configuration. Playback will commence playing the samples provided from the reader until either the reader returns an error, or the context signals done.

<a name="StreamConfig"></a>

## type [StreamConfig](<https://github.com/verkada/malgo/blob/master/_examples/io_api/stream_config.go#L7-L11>)

StreamConfig describes the parameters for an audio stream. Default values will pick the defaults of the default device.

```go
type StreamConfig struct {
    Format     malgo.FormatType
    Channels   int
    SampleRate int
}
```

# playback

```go
import "github.com/gen2brain/malgo/_examples/playback"
```
